items.title: Is DZ Mafia a French drug cartel, an illicit 'brand', or a drug trafficking 'franchise'?
items.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Yann Bisiou, Senior Lecturer in Private Law and Criminal Law at Paul-Valéry University. The threats against the mayor of Arles illustrate how criminal networks are becoming increasingly brazen, challenging authority head-on. Rather than focusing solely on the violence, public drug policy specialist Bissiot argues that the incident exposes an institutional failure. He asserts that DZ Mafia is not a policing problem but rather the predictable consequence of a prohibition model failing to reduce a lucrative illicit drug trade.

items.title: Spain battles spreading wildfires fuelled by climate-change driven heat
items.description: Wildfires in Spain have forced authorities to evacuate several districts around the city of Toledo, 75 miles from the Spanish capital Madrid. It's one of many wildfires ravaging across western Europe as parts of southern France and Italy are also ablaze. In 2025, more than 393,000 hectares had been consumed by flames in Spain, according to the European Forest Fire Information System. It is the worst toll in the country's recent history, fuelled by extreme temperatures cause by climate change.

items.description: In the world of artificial intelligence, frontier models are displaying ever-more sophisticated autonomous behaviour. The incident, once again underlining the growing threat advanced AIs pose to cybersecurity.

items.title: How the student 'Cockroach' movement grew its influence across India
items.description: Young Indians are keeping up the pressure - continuing their demands for the Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan - to step down. This as their online satirical group the "Cockroach People's Party" grows in stature and support. It won millions of followers on social media and tens of thousands of sign-ups since its launch in May.

items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials.
